stem: A web performance study measures the response time (ms) for a sample of 20 website users. The sample mean is 135.7 ms with a sample standard deviation of 35.7 ms. However, 2 of the 20 observations appear to be outliers (values more than 2.5 standard deviations from the mean). If these outliers are removed, the remaining 18 observations have a mean of 131.3 ms and standard deviation of 27.5 ms. Estimate the true population mean and provide a 95% confidence interval that accounts for the uncertainty about whether outliers should be included.

ground_truth.answer: With all data: mean = 135.7, 95% CI = [119.0, 152.4]. Without outliers: mean = 131.3, 95% CI = [118.3, 144.3]. Combined interval accounting for analytic uncertainty: [118.3, 152.4]. Point estimate: 133.5 ms.

solution_steps: The outlier treatment choice creates structural uncertainty. Combined interval: [118.3, 152.4].
common_failure_modes: Using only the full sample without considering outlier impact
Removing outliers without reporting both analyses
Not recognizing that the choice of analysis method is itself a source of uncertainty
